summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-structured, covering all major requirements: INI parsing, comments, multi-line values, type coercion (int/float/bool/string), schema validation (required keys/sections, type errors, optional keys), JSON and YAML output, and full round-trip integration. The nine test classes map cleanly onto TDD cycles, fixtures (basic_ini, basic_schema, tmp_path) are well-scoped and reused appropriately, and docstrings clearly document intent. Boolean variants (true/yes/on/1, etc.) are exhaustively checked. Gaps that prevent a 5 in rigor: no test for invalid boolean coercion (e.g., 'maybe' → boolean should raise MigrationError), the unknown schema type code path is untested, keys appearing before any section header are not exercised, and there are no tests for duplicate keys/sections or Windows-style CRLF line endings. The inline-comment stripping logic also has a subtle space-before-semicolon condition that is tested but not stress-tested with boundary spacing variants. Despite these gaps, the suite would catch the vast majority of regressions in the implementation.
